content: Headache treatment depends on type. Tension-type headache: acute treatment with simple analgesics (acetaminophen, NSAIDs); avoid opioids. Chronic TTH prevention: amitriptyline (first-line, 10-75mg at bedtime), mirtazapine, venlafaxine; non-pharmacologic (stress management, physical therapy, acupuncture, biofeedback). Cluster headache acute treatment: high-flow oxygen (100% at 12-15 L/min via non-rebreather for 15-20 minutes—first-line, safe, effective), subcutaneous sumatriptan 6mg (rapid onset, can repeat once in 24 hours), intranasal sumatriptan or zolmitriptan. Cluster prevention: verapamil (first-line, start 80mg TID, titrate up to 480-960mg/day, requires ECG monitoring for heart block), galcanezumab (monthly injection for episodic cluster), lithium (second-line, monitor levels/thyroid/renal), topiramate. Transitional prevention: prednisone taper (60mg × 5 days then taper) or greater occipital nerve block (steroid + local anesthetic) to bridge until preventive takes effect. Refractory cases: occipital nerve stimulation, sphenopalatine ganglion stimulation, deep brain stimulation (hypothalamus—last resort). Medication overuse headache: withdraw offending medication (abruptly for triptans/NSAIDs, taper for opioids/benzodiazepines), bridge with steroids/NSAIDs, initiate preventive.
